entities.name: Patient ID
entities.definition: Unique patient identification number in the system
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient identification number, typically composed of digits and/or letters

entities.name: Last Name
entities.definition: Current family name of the patient
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's family name as it appears in the document

entities.name: Maiden Name
entities.definition: Birth family name of the patient, if different from current name
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's birth name if mentioned and different from current name

entities.name: First Name
entities.definition: Given name(s) of the patient
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's given name(s) as they appear in the document

entities.name: Gender
entities.definition: Patient's gender
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the patient's gender: Female, Male

entities.name: Date of Birth
entities.definition: Complete birth date of the patient
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the date of birth in Day/Month/Year format (DD/MM/YYYY) or Month/Day/Year format (MM/DD/YYYY)

entities.name: Hospital
entities.definition: Reference healthcare institution
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the name of the hospital or healthcare facility

entities.name: Diagnosis Date
entities.definition: Date of anatomopathological biopsy - official cancer diagnosis date
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the date of anatomopathological biopsy, which corresponds to the official cancer diagnosis date. Look for the examination date or biopsy reception date in pathology reports. Format: day/month/year (DD/MM/YYYY) or month/day/year (MM/DD/YYYY).
entities.aggregation_instructions: If multiple biopsy dates are found, return the earliest one as it corresponds to the initial diagnosis.

entities.name: Cancer Location
entities.definition: Anatomical location of the primary tumor (primary cancer site).
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the location of the PRIMARY tumor. Look for expressions like 'primary tumor', 'primary cancer', 'primary site', 'cancer origin'. Ignore metastases, secondary locations, or dissemination.
entities.aggregation_instructions: If multiple mentions of primary location are found, return the most frequently mentioned or most recently confirmed. Map to a single value from the valid_values list. If no clear primary location, return 'Not determined'.

entities.name: Primary Lesion
entities.definition: Primary lesion on CT/MRI imaging - characteristics of the primary tumor on imaging
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the description of the primary lesion as described in the latest imaging report (CT or MRI). Include size, characteristics, and precise location.

entities.name: Complementary Assessment
entities.definition: Distant metastatic workup - results of complementary examinations
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract results of distant staging workup (CT scan, MRI, PET-scan, nuclear medicine). Focus on examination conclusions for key information about metastases.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Synthesize conclusions from different complementary examinations. Organize by examination type and date. Highlight metastatic sites and evolution.

entities.name: Primary Tumor Commentary
entities.definition: Clinical synthesis of primary tumor history, evolution and treatments received.
entities.extraction_instructions: Create a chronological synthesis focused on: initial diagnosis, tumor characteristics, oncological treatments, therapeutic responses, disease progressions. EXCLUDE details of palliative care, family information, and non-oncological aspects.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Structure chronologically: 1) Initial diagnosis and characteristics, 2) Adjuvant treatments, 3) Relapses and metastatic treatments, 4) Disease evolution. Maintain oncological focus. Synthesize in short, clear paragraphs.

entities.name: Molecular Abnormality
entities.definition: All molecular alterations detected in the tumor, including genetic mutations, losses, amplifications, variants of uncertain significance and specific biomarkers.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract all molecular abnormalities, genetic mutations, gene losses, amplifications, variants of uncertain significance (VUS), and biomarker results reported in clinical sources. Include gene name, alteration type, and any notes on clinical significance if available.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Group and list each unique molecular abnormality in a structured way (e.g.: gene, alteration type, functional status). Organize the list by alteration type (mutations, amplifications, losses, others). Eliminate any redundancy and ensure clarity and readability of results.

entities.name: Metastatic Status
entities.definition: Indicates whether the cancer has metastases based on documented evidence and includes verbatim text used for this conclusion.
entities.extraction_instructions: Look for explicit evidence of metastases in the text: mentions of 'metastases', 'metastatic', 'M1', 'dissemination', 'secondary locations', or specific organs with metastases. Extract exact text containing this information.
entities.aggregation_instructions: If evidence of metastases is found, return 'Yes' followed by verbatim text in quotes, for example: 'Yes - "Presence of multiple hepatic metastases"

entities.name: Metastatic Sites
entities.definition: Metastatic sites and their clinical evolution over time.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract metastatic sites with their clinical evolution (progression, stability, regression) and dates if available. Include new lesions, progression, regression.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Format: [Date] - [Site]: [Evolution/Description]. Classify chronologically. If no date, group under 'Documented sites'. Merge identical mentions of the same site.

entities.name: Histological Type
entities.definition: Precise histological classification of the tumor according to its cellular type
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the specific histological type of the tumor as mentioned in the pathology report
entities.aggregation_instructions: Keep all mentioned histological types, removing duplicates, organized in reverse chronological order (most recent first), prioritizing the most recent and detailed

entities.name: Location
entities.definition: Precise anatomical location of the tumor
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the detailed anatomical location of the primary tumor

entities.name: Previous Chemotherapy
entities.definition: Chemotherapy treatments previously administered to the patient
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract all details of completed chemotherapies, including protocols, dates and results if mentioned
entities.aggregation_instructions: Analyze all mentions of chemotherapies and create a deduplicated chronological list. Remove exact duplicates and consolidate similar protocols. Organize in reverse chronological order (most recent first). Format: • Protocol/drug (date) - indication/context if relevant. Include details of cycles, clinical trials and administration modalities.

entities.name: Previous Radiotherapy
entities.definition: Radiotherapy treatments previously administered to the patient
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract all details of completed radiotherapies, including location, doses and dates if mentioned
entities.aggregation_instructions: Analyze all mentions of radiotherapies and create a deduplicated chronological list. Remove exact duplicates and consolidate similar treatments. Organize in reverse chronological order (most recent first). Format: • Radiotherapy type (date) - location/indication if relevant. Include details of doses, durations and specific modalities.

entities.name: Previous Surgery
entities.definition: Surgical interventions previously performed on the patient with clinical descriptions.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract surgical interventions with understandable clinical descriptions (e.g.: 'mastectomy', 'resection', 'removal', 'lymph node dissection'). IGNORE procedure codes, technical codes, and alphanumeric references. Look for descriptions in clear medical language. Include date and anatomical location if mentioned.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Extract only surgeries with clear clinical descriptions. Format: [Date] - [Surgical description] ([Location]). Ignore all technical codes (procedure codes, alphanumeric). Remove duplicates. Classify in descending chronological order. If only technical codes are present, return 'Surgeries documented only by technical codes - clinical details not available'.

entities.name: Performance Status (WHO)
entities.definition: Patient's performance status score according to World Health Organization scale (0-4)
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract the WHO performance status score, typically expressed as a number from 0 to 4

entities.name: G8 Score
entities.definition: G8 geriatric assessment score for patients over 70 years old (score out of 17).
entities.extraction_instructions: Explicitly look for 'G8 Score', 'G8', or 'G8 geriatric assessment' followed by a numerical value (e.g.: 'G8: 12/17', 'G8 Score = 15'). Also extract any mention of absence of G8 evaluation or reasons why it was not performed.
entities.aggregation_instructions: If a G8 score is found, return 'G8 Score: [value]/17'. If no score is documented but the patient is over 70 years old, return 'G8 Score not documented (eligible patient)'. If the patient is under 70 years old, return 'G8 Score not applicable (patient < 70 years)'. If an alternative assessment is mentioned, indicate it.

entities.name: Notable Personal History
entities.definition: Significant personal medical history of the patient (excluding current cancer)
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract ONLY clinically significant personal medical history DISTINCT from the currently treated cancer. INCLUDE: other prior cancers, chronic diseases impacting prognosis/treatment (diabetes, heart/kidney/liver failure, COPD), major surgical interventions, drug allergies. EXCLUDE: isolated benign symptoms, routine consultations, normal examinations, current cancer details.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Create a list hierarchized by clinical importance: 1) Other prior cancers (with dates) 2) Major comorbidities impacting prognosis 3) Drug allergies 4) Major surgical interventions. Eliminate non-essential details. Format: short points, one line per significant history item.

entities.name: Family History
entities.definition: Family medical history relevant to the oncological context.
entities.extraction_instructions: Extract ONLY clinically relevant family pathologies: family cancers, hereditary syndromes, hereditary genetic mutations. PRIORITY to cancers of the same type or predisposing syndromes. Look for 'father/mother/brother/sister with', 'family history of cancer', 'family history'. EXCLUDE non-oncological family pathologies unless directly related to cancer risk.
entities.aggregation_instructions: Hierarchize by oncological relevance: 1) Same type cancers in close relatives 2) Other family cancers 3) Hereditary syndromes/mutations. Format: [Relation] - [Pathology] (age if mentioned). If no relevant history, return 'No oncological family history documented'.
